content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of length NNN and a non-negative integer mmm, we define the function fm(A)f_m(A)fm‚Äã(A) as

fm(A)=‚àëi=1NAi‚ãÖimf_m(A) = \sum_{i=1}^N A_i \cdot i^mfm‚Äã(A)=i=1‚àëN‚ÄãAi‚Äã‚ãÖimYou are given two integers NNN and CCC.

A sequence AAA of non-negative integers of length KKK is said to be good if it satisfies the following four conditions:


1‚â§K‚â§N1 \leq K \leq N1‚â§K‚â§N
0‚â§A1‚â§A2‚â§A3‚â§‚Ä¶‚â§AK0 \le A_1 \leq A_2\leq A_3 \leq \ldots \leq A_K0‚â§A1‚Äã‚â§A2‚Äã‚â§A3‚Äã‚â§‚Ä¶‚â§AK‚Äã
f0(A)=Nf_0(A) = Nf0‚Äã(A)=N
f1(A)=Cf_1(A) = Cf1‚Äã(A)=C
If there is no sequence that is good, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.

Otherwise, let MMM denote the minimum possible value of f2(A)f_2(A)f2‚Äã(A) across all good sequences.

Compute MMM, and also construct any good sequence AAA for which f2(A)=Mf_2(A) = Mf2‚Äã(A)=M.

If there are multiple possible good sequences for which f2(A)=Mf_2(A) = Mf2‚Äã(A)=M, you may find any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, which contains two space-separated integers NNN and CCC.


Output Format
For each test case:



If there is no sequence that satisfies the conditions, print a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
If a good sequence exists,

On the first line, print a single integer ‚Äî the minimum possible value of f2(A)f_2(A)f2‚Äã(A) across all good sequences.
On the second line, print the length of the sequence AAA you will construct - a single integer KKK.
On the third line, print KKK space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AKA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_KA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AK‚Äã, denoting the sequence you constructed.



If there are multiple valid sequences, you may print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 10610^{6}106.
